checks.a_analysis: The output JSON faithfully represents the text found in the source document. All headings, legend items, and the sources paragraph correspond directly to the text visible in the map. No invented content or fabricated sections were found.
There is no contact information (phone numbers, emails, URLs) in the source document or the output JSON.
The single-page document is fully represented. The title, date, legend, and sources are all present as structural elements. The map and logo are included as image elements.
There are no tables in the source document.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The text content of the legend and sources is captured in full. While the labels for the north arrow ('N') and the scale bar ('0 25 50 100 Miles') are not present as separate text elements, they are part of the graphical representation of the map, which is captured via the image element. The county labels on the map are similarly part of the map graphic. The omission of these few words as distinct text elements does not constitute a significant (25%+) omission of the document's textual content.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: All extracted text matches the source document exactly. No wording changes, date alterations, or number discrepancies were found. The legend counts and dates are perfectly preserved.

checks.a_analysis: The output was reviewed for any repeated text blocks. The title, date, legend headers, and sources appear only once in the output, matching their occurrence in the source document. The OCR text contains many map labels (county names) which are correctly omitted from the semantic text structure as they are part of the map image itself.

The heading hierarchy in the output skips from level 2 ('June 25, 2025') to level 4 ('Parcel Updates:'). According to the binary check definitions, a skip from H2 to H4 is a defect as it breaks the logical structure required for screen reader navigation. There is no H3 heading provided to bridge the gap between the document subtitle and the section header.
checks.b_evidence_source: The document transitions from the date sub-header to the legend section header 'Parcel Updates:'.

checks.a_analysis: The source document does not contain any tables. The map legend is presented as a list of text items with associated color blocks, which the pipeline has correctly interpreted as a list rather than a table structure.

The reading order in the output flows logically from the top-level titles and date, to the legend and sources on the left, and finally to the main map and logo. This reflects a standard and accessible interpretation of the visual layout of the map document, ensuring that context (the legend and sources) is provided before or alongside the graphic data.

The map legend, which contains a series of update statuses and counts, has been converted into an unordered list. This is an appropriate structural choice that preserves the meaning and grouping of the legend items for accessibility purposes.
The source document contains no hyperlinks, URLs, or email addresses. No links were injected or missed in the output.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML document organizes the content into a single-column layout which is consistent with accessible web design. The hierarchy of information—starting with the title, then the date, followed by the parcel update counts and sources—matches the logical flow of the original document. While the map and legend are converted from a graphical representation to a textual list and descriptive placeholders, the visual organization of the information remains recognizable and logical.

There are no traditional tables in the source PDF. The legend, which is a key-value list of updates, is rendered as a standard bulleted list in the HTML. This effectively communicates the data without visual corruption.

The source PDF's primary content is a large map of North Carolina and an NCGICC logo. In the HTML output, both of these meaningful images are missing, represented only by broken image placeholders with alt text. Additionally, the north arrow and scale bar from the map are completely absent without placeholders. Since the map is the core informational content of the document, its failure to render is a major defect.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains a large color-coded map of North Carolina counties and a logo in the bottom right corner (NCGICC logo).
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows broken image icons for the map and the logo: '![A map of North Carolina showing the project status...]' and '![NCGICC logo]'.
checks.b_evidence_location: Main body content area, following the Sources section.

checks.a_analysis: No body content is duplicated. The document titles and section headers appear in both the navigation Table of Contents (Contents sidebar) and the main body, which is standard accessible design and not considered a defect.
